semantic_sweep.review_note: Reviewed the complete chapter, all eight assigned source notes and mapping limits, the public SCF schema and 3-valid/6-invalid corpus, readiness/residual boundary, 2-valid/6-invalid lifecycle probe, all 25 Lean theorem declarations grouped under four manifest targets, every manifest atom, every prose scanner candidate, diagrams, status language, test plan, source crosswalk, summary, and handoff. The sweep replaced a generic capability slot with a versioned consumer-relative substitution contract; separated observable and failure semantics from interface compatibility, authority from raw candidate power, provenance from adequacy, local qualification from universal substitutability, rollback metadata from effect-complete recovery, and finite record proofs from empirical field preservation; promoted evaluator dependencies, lease decay, field-owned regression and incident memory, downstream reliance, state and composition duties, adversarial failure taxonomy, and a matched causal-transfer campaign into manifest ownership; and preserved zero support-state effect.

scope.population: Prospectively declared capability-field versions and replacement candidates spanning models, weights, prompts, policies, tools, adapters, data or retrieval snapshots, evaluators, dependencies, hardware, runtimes, state schemas, routes, consumers, and update types.
scope.environment: The ASI Stack public schema, deterministic fixtures, finite Lean predicates, and future frozen natural/adversarial workloads with exact artifacts, dependencies, environments, threat models, consumers, uses, evaluators, authority topology, state, side effects, regressions, incidents, monitors, and cost rules; production deployment is outside current evidence.
scope.model: A versioned consumer-relative substitution-contract and lifecycle state machine whose current implementation validates record shape, selected route consequences, transition constraints, fixture counts, and no-promotion boundaries; future preservation claims require real candidate behavior and independent observers.
scope.authority: Field governance owns semantic identity and versioning; System Authority bounds grants; consumers declare use requirements; separate-enough evaluators and promotion owners judge evidence; execution owns effects; incident and rollback owners govern recovery; Evidence States alone owns support movement.
scope.time: Semantic review current through 2026-07-14; field, candidate, dependencies, environment, evaluator, benchmark epoch, threat model, consumer, use, lease, incidents, regressions, state, reliance, monitors, authority, effects, and costs must be frozen and requalified after material change.
scope.artifact: Chapter, manifest, eight source notes and mappings, SCF schema, nine record fixtures, readiness/residual harness, lifecycle trace probe, 25 Lean theorem declarations under four proof targets, field history, qualification and replacement receipts, effect inventory, and downstream public projections.

assumptions: A field's consumer-visible behavior, failures, abstentions, side effects, resource/privacy/security effects, authority, state, and evidence duties can be specified precisely enough to test without claiming complete semantic identity.

Qualification scope and decay, evaluator dependencies, downstream reliance, migration, composition, and recovery effects can be observed prospectively by implementations independent enough for the declared claim.
Strong conventional release-engineering comparators can be frozen and evaluated on the same usefulness, safety, latency, resource, labor, governance, recovery, and residual frontier.
chapter_defaults.strongest_counterclaim: Existing typed interfaces, versioning, provenance, capability security, regression suites, canaries, and rollback practice already provide the useful replacement boundary; SCFs add an untestable semantic label and governance burden without preserving more real behavior or authority.
chapter_defaults.falsifier: Any admitted field route violates a frozen observable, failure, authority, qualification, evaluator, lifecycle, history, state, composition, reliance, or recovery obligation; or a strong simpler comparator dominates the SCF condition on the preregistered joint frontier and transfer set.

required_lanes.necessity: Freeze SCF project lineage and current capability-security, versioning, provenance, ML release, behavioral-refinement, canary, rollback, and model-registry comparators without novelty laundering.
Justify field ownership, material-change classification, consumer standing, authority ceilings, evaluator separation, acceptable residuals, deprecation, retirement, and recovery cost.
Specify finite identity, refinement, lease, authority, lifecycle, regression, reliance, migration, composition, terminal-state, receipt, and recovery obligations while exposing model limits.
Exercise every field with valid and rejecting records, exact artifact locks, downstream consumers, effect inventories, drift and capture controls, migration, monitoring, and rehearsed recovery.

Measure preservation, failures, unauthorized effects, evaluator calibration and capture, migration and composition, recovery, useful throughput, missed help, latency, resources, labor, governance cost, and residuals.
Ablate identity, authority, lease, evaluator separation, regression memory, composition requalification, migration accounting, and effect-complete recovery under matched candidates.
Repeat across models, vendors, tools, data, hardware, runtimes, domains, consumers, threat models, authority topologies, statefulness, side effects, and update types with independent implementations.

contrary_evidence: Typed APIs, model registries, SemVer, provenance systems, capability security, regression suites, staged rollout, feature flags, and rollback systems are mature prior-art families covering substantial parts of the proposal.
No finite schema can completely define natural-language capability meaning, and consumer-relative contracts can fragment identity or hide omitted stakeholders and behaviors.
Independent evaluators can share data, incentives, models, benchmarks, organizational control, and blind spots; declared separation can be theater.
A candidate can pass frozen regressions while failing rare, adaptive, compositional, stateful, or open-world cases, and stronger suites can intensify benchmark overfitting.
Effect-complete rollback may be impossible after external actions, disclosure, learned updates, descendant creation, irreversible state migration, or reliance by other systems.

The current evidence is internally authored schemas, deterministic fixtures, and finite proofs; it includes no real candidate pair, provenance workflow, migration, composition, deployed route, or executed rollback.
known_confounds: Model capability, prompt and tool quality, data contamination, dependency changes, hardware, load, randomness, and task mix can mimic field-preservation effects.
Contract authors can choose observable partitions and thresholds that favor the candidate or omit rare and affected-consumer failures.
Candidate and evaluator may share training data, model family, organization, benchmark, prompts, or hidden assumptions despite nominal separation.
Canary exposure, fallback frequency, refusal, and task exclusion can improve observed safety while reducing coverage and useful throughput.
Migration fidelity, privacy, and rollback success depend on state visibility, external systems, time, and irreversibility beyond the active implementation artifact.
Governance labor, incident response, evaluator maintenance, and residual custody can be omitted from cost or attributed to another layer.
A component may preserve its isolated contract while changed timing, dependencies, routes, or neighboring fields cause composition failure.
acceptance_criteria: Every declared field obligation has an exact producer, consumer, evaluator, artifact lock, valid case, rejecting mutation, residual owner, and exercised consequence; seeded drift, capture, stale lease, regression loss, authority widening, migration, composition, leakage, and recovery failures reach exact non-default outcomes.

A preregistered real-candidate campaign meets joint preservation, safety, usefulness, cost, recovery, causal-ablation, replication, and transfer gates against strong conventional comparators with independent implementations and evaluators.
narrowing_criteria: Narrow every result to the exact field version, candidates, artifacts, dependencies, environment, threat model, consumers, uses, evaluator, epoch, authority, state, regressions, incidents, monitors, and costs.

Separate schema validity, interface compatibility, provenance, behavioral refinement, qualification, route permission, evaluator independence, migration fidelity, composition safety, rollback readiness, rollback execution, support movement, and deployed outcomes.
Unknown or failed identity, authority, evidence, lease, evaluator, state, composition, reliance, or recovery obligations remain shadow, canary, quarantine, deprecated, rejected, or residual.
refutation_criteria: Refute an invariant when a reachable admitted trace violates it or a required field lacks an exercised producer, consumer, or consequence.
Refute a mechanism benefit when its matched ablation preserves every frozen outcome and cost gate or the full mechanism worsens the joint frontier.
Refute superiority or transfer when a strong simpler comparator dominates or the effect fails across the declared model, workload, consumer, environment, threat, and update set.
deprecation_criteria: Deprecate only when a more precise capability-identity, versioning, provenance, authority, replacement, migration, composition, recovery, evidence, or runtime owner supersedes the atom and every consumer migrates with lineage.

residual.next_unblocking_condition: Freeze a real two-implementation field and strong registry/versioning/provenance/canary/rollback comparators; exact artifacts and environments; partitioned natural/adversarial workloads; independent contract, authority, evaluator, migration, composition, and recovery observers; joint metrics; effect inventory; causal ablations; replication; and transfer after P2/P3 semantics exist.

non_claims: Semantic review, source mapping, schema validity, a passing fixture, a finite trace, or a Lean theorem does not establish semantic equivalence, safe composition, evaluator independence, provenance, production enforcement, successful rollback, or superiority.

The current 3/6 record corpus, 2/6 lifecycle probe, selected readiness/residual checks, and 25 theorem declarations establish only their exact synthetic record, count, route, and finite-predicate consequences.
No support state, route authority, deployment permission, release status, AGI, or ASI claim changes without its separate owner and accepted evidence transition.

stable-capability-fields.core.strongest_counterclaim: Ordinary typed registries, versioning, provenance, tests, canaries, and rollback practice can supply every useful substitution control with less governance overhead; the proposed field record neither defines semantic identity objectively nor prevents a captured organization from blessing drift.
stable-capability-fields.core.falsifier: A reachable admitted route violates any bound observable, authority, qualification, history, migration, lifecycle, or recovery obligation; or a strong simpler comparator dominates the frozen joint frontier across all declared transfer settings.
acceptance_criteria: Exercise the exact proposition with valid and rejecting cases, independent-enough observation, downstream consumer assertions, explicit residuals, and no inference beyond the frozen field, candidate, consumer, use, environment, threat model, evaluator, epoch, and authority scope.

stable-capability-fields.problem.001.strongest_counterclaim: Existing software and ML release practice already makes consequential implementation changes observable enough for every relevant consumer, so a separate consumer-relative field contract repairs no material gap.
stable-capability-fields.problem.001.falsifier: A dated, representative audit finds no material hidden semantic, authority, evidence, reliance, migration, or rollback drift under current replacement practice.
acceptance_criteria: A dated comparator audit preserves the strongest existing approach, demonstrates the exact unresolved boundary, and avoids implying that the proposed remedy works.

stable-capability-fields.insufficiency.0.strongest_counterclaim: A well-engineered combination of API compatibility, provenance, benchmark regression, capability security, canaries, and rollback runbooks supplies every named property without the proposed SCF abstraction.
stable-capability-fields.insufficiency.0.falsifier: A frozen strong comparator implements and exercises every named identity, behavioral, authority, evaluator, lease, regression, reliance, migration, composition, and effect-recovery boundary at equal or lower joint cost.
acceptance_criteria: A dated comparator audit preserves the strongest existing approach, demonstrates the exact unresolved boundary, and avoids implying that the proposed remedy works.

stable-capability-fields.minimum.001.strongest_counterclaim: The current minimum is self-authored schema and finite-state theater that demonstrates no real equivalence, evaluator independence, migration, composition, authority enforcement, or recovery.
stable-capability-fields.minimum.001.falsifier: Any stated count, command, fixture classification, theorem grouping, or non-claim fails clean reproduction, or prose infers real behavior from finite records.
acceptance_criteria: Reproduce 3 valid and 6 expected-invalid SCF records, 2 valid and 6 expected-invalid lifecycle traces, the selected readiness/residual routes, 25 Lean theorem declarations, four proof-target groups, and every non-claim from a clean environment.

stable-capability-fields.beyond_sota.001.strongest_counterclaim: The full SCF condition adds evaluator and governance cost but is dominated by simpler release engineering on useful throughput, failures, recovery, and residual risk.
stable-capability-fields.beyond_sota.001.falsifier: The full condition misses its preregistered preservation, safety, usefulness, cost, causal-ablation, replication, or transfer gate, or a simpler comparator dominates it.
acceptance_criteria: A preregistered multi-field campaign with real candidates, strong comparators, independent implementations and evaluators, causal ablations, joint metrics, replication, and transfer meets every frozen promotion gate without hiding negative/null results.

stable-capability-fields.mechanism.001.strongest_counterclaim: This mechanism is unnecessary, unimplementable, or fails to causally improve the declared substitution boundary: Define field identity independently of any implementation, model family, vendor, prompt, tool, benchmark, deployment, or route name; a material semantic change creates a governed field version or a new field rather than silently reusing the old identity.
stable-capability-fields.mechanism.001.falsifier: A matched ablation preserves every frozen outcome and cost gate, or an exercised trace shows the mechanism cannot enforce the stated boundary.
acceptance_criteria: Exercise the exact proposition with valid and rejecting cases, independent-enough observation, downstream consumer assertions, explicit residuals, and no inference beyond the frozen field, candidate, consumer, use, environment, threat model, evaluator, epoch, and authority scope.

stable-capability-fields.mechanism.002.falsifier: A matched ablation preserves every frozen outcome and cost gate, or an exercised trace shows the mechanism cannot enforce the stated boundary.

stable-capability-fields.prose.553a50cdf.rationale: Line-wrapped Reflexive Router integration restates the stable-field interface already owned by structured atoms; it creates no independent material claim.

stable-capability-fields.prose.383c31ff4.rationale: Explicit limitation fragment narrows inference from the dispatch fixture and creates no independent material claim.

stable-capability-fields.prose.1dc6f1caf.rationale: Governance-continuity motivation for the core substitution contract.

stable-capability-fields.prose.dccef6b5a.rationale: Reader-language restatement of the owned replacement-identity problem.

stable-capability-fields.prose.843dc14fe.rationale: Cross-chapter reading-path description, not a new SCF claim.

stable-capability-fields.prose.7787a307c.rationale: Reader-language restatement of the technical continuity problem.

stable-capability-fields.prose.0824182e7.rationale: Human-language restatement of the core non-drift objective.

stable-capability-fields.prose.f0f2b554f.rationale: Consumer-relative scope is owned by the core contract and qualification mechanisms.

stable-capability-fields.prose.75a0a1bda.rationale: Explicitly bounded description of what a contract record can expose, without efficacy inference.

stable-capability-fields.prose.d73042d9c.rationale: Question-form elaboration of the behavioral-refinement admission mechanism.

stable-capability-fields.prose.32a142bf8.rationale: Inline labeled duplicate of the manifest-owned core atom.

stable-capability-fields.prose.966e1f5f8.rationale: Behavioral-refinement definition owned by candidate-admission mechanism.

stable-capability-fields.prose.3af9edf56.rationale: Separation-of-duties obligation owned by evaluator mechanism.

stable-capability-fields.prose.a5c5ad3ad.rationale: Consequence of field-owned regression and incident memory.

stable-capability-fields.prose.6be349d0a.rationale: Schema-boundary caveat, not an additional positive claim.

stable-capability-fields.prose.77fb22d14.rationale: Finite-evidence ceiling owned by the twelfth invariant.

stable-capability-fields.prose.5b8b20bc5.rationale: Prose formatting of benchmark-overfitting failure mode.

stable-capability-fields.prose.1d16a12f3.rationale: Prose formatting of rollback-theater failure mode.

stable-capability-fields.prose.860618b77.rationale: Transition sentence introducing distinct controls and measurements.

stable-capability-fields.prose.8c69fadb8.rationale: Schema limitation and outcome-evidence boundary owned by the twelfth invariant.

stable-capability-fields.prose.5dd3fb2e5.rationale: Transfer dimensions owned by the beyond-SOTA campaign.

stable-capability-fields.prose.1e5fc812a.rationale: Report of the current finite Lean module's derived consequences, already owned by formal targets.

stable-capability-fields.prose.6d2f13d7f.rationale: Handoff sentence assigning the next chapter's transaction role.

non_claims: Completing this semantic sweep changes decomposition and ownership metadata only; it does not establish proposition truth or promote support.
Every current executable result remains bounded to exact public fixtures, validators, finite Lean predicates, and stated non-claims.
No real candidate equivalence, evaluator independence, deployed authority enforcement, migration, composition, rollback, or beyond-SOTA result is claimed.
